title: AArch64 PAC return-address signing macro removed from default defines
problem: The default compile defines no longer include `-DHITLS_AARCH64_PACIASP`, so `AARCH64_PACIASP/AARCH64_AUTIASP` expand to no-ops in assembly (`crypt_arm.h`). This removes return-address signing hardening for AArch64 assembly crypto code.

title: x2 SHAKE path is endianness-incorrect on big-endian AArch64
problem: The new NEON x2 SHAKE absorb/squeeze path loads/stores 64-bit lanes as raw bytes without LE conversion. Existing SHA3 ARMv8 assembly handles `HITLS_BIG_ENDIAN`, but this new C path does not, producing wrong SHAKE output on big-endian targets.

title: ML-KEM switched from provider-aware hash dispatch to hardwired local SHA3/SHAKE
problem: Keygen/encrypt now call direct one-shot SHA3/SHAKE functions, bypassing `ctx->libCtx` provider dispatch and its error path. This breaks consistency with provider-based operation used elsewhere in the file and can violate provider/FIPS routing expectations.

title: Noise PRF path ignores `libCtx` and cannot report SHAKE provider errors
problem: `SampleEta1/2` now use direct `CRYPT_SHAKE256` one-shot calls and always return success, bypassing provider dispatch (`ctx->libCtx`) and removing error propagation previously provided by EAL MD calls.

title: Sensitive buffer cleanse length not updated after buffer size increase
problem: `kr` was expanded from 64 to 65 bytes, but cleansing still wipes only 64 bytes. One byte remains uncleansed on stack.
